credential_capture.label: Slack workspace credentials
credential_capture.description: Paste the session credentials from your Slack browser session. These are captured and sealed per-connection; the deployment does not need env vars.
credential_capture.submit_label: Create Slack connection and start first sync

fields.name: slack_workspace
fields.label: Slack workspace domain
fields.help_text: Enter just the subdomain — e.g. 'myteam' from myteam.slack.com.

fields.name: slack_token
fields.label: Slack session token (xoxc-…)
fields.help_text: Copy the xoxc-… token from your browser's Slack JS context. See the runbook link for exact steps.

fields.name: slack_cookie
fields.label: Slack 'd' cookie
fields.help_text: Copy the 'd' cookie from your browser while logged in to Slack. Same runbook as above.

refresh_policy.rationale: Slack credentials are durable and the Docker reference deployment includes the slackdump runtime; refresh moderately.

streams.name: workspace
streams.description: Authenticated Slack workspace: team identity, authenticated user/bot identity, enterprise affiliation, primary URL.

display.label: Slack workspace
display.detail: Workspace name, domain, team ID, the authenticated user (whose session this is), and enterprise parent (if any).

streams.name: channels
streams.description: Public/private channels, DMs, and group DMs visible to the authenticated user. Structural and identity fields only; member count is in the channel_stats stream.

display.label: Your Slack channels
display.detail: Channel name, type flags (is_im/is_mpim/is_private/is_shared/is_archived), creator, topic and purpose (with last-set time), created-at. Member counts are in channel_stats.

streams.name: channel_stats
streams.description: Sampled member counts per channel, keyed by {channel_id}:{YYYY-MM-DD}. One record per channel per calendar day (UTC); re-running on the same day is idempotent. Preserves a time series of membership volume without creating false entity-version churn on the channels stream.

display.label: Slack channel member counts (daily)
display.detail: Daily snapshot of num_members for each channel. Each record covers one calendar day.

streams.name: channel_memberships
streams.description: Channel ↔ user edges where slackdump could enumerate members.

display.label: Channel memberships
display.detail: For each channel we could see the membership of, one row per user. Includes DMs (channel member is the DM counterpart).

streams.name: users
streams.description: Users (human + bot) visible to the authenticated principal.

display.label: People and bots in your Slack workspace
display.detail: Username, real name, display name, email (when visible), timezone, bot flag, admin/owner flags, deletion status.

streams.name: messages
streams.description: All messages across visible channels. Thread structure preserved via thread_ts. Slack marks deletions with subtype='tombstone' so messages stream is effectively mutable despite the append-only semantic class.

display.label: Your Slack messages
display.detail: Channel, author, timestamp, text, thread parent, reply count + reply_user_ids (who replied), edited-at, client_msg_id (dedup), subtype (joins/leaves/bot/tombstone), pinned status, block-kit metadata.

streams.name: message_attachments
streams.description: Link-preview unfurls and bot-generated rich attachments inlined on messages. One row per (message, attachment index).

display.label: Link previews and message attachments
display.detail: Unfurled links (URL, title, description, source service, thumbnail) and bot-generated rich attachments on messages.

display.label: Slack reactions
display.detail: One row per (message, emoji, user). Emoji name, reacting user, reacted-to message.

streams.name: files
streams.description: File attachments referenced in messages. Metadata only by default.

display.label: Files shared in Slack
display.detail: Filename, mimetype, size, upload time, uploader, public/private flags, title.

streams.name: canvases
streams.description: Slack Canvas documents attached to channels (mimetype application/vnd.slack-docs, filetype 'quip'). Archive captures canvas metadata; content bodies live behind files.slack.com authenticated URLs and are not materialized here.

display.label: Slack canvases
display.detail: For each channel canvas: canvas file ID, host channel, author, size, is_empty flag, quip thread ID (Slack's internal doc ID), title, created/updated time, permalink. Document bodies are not in this export.

streams.name: stars
streams.description: Messages, files, or channels the user has starred/saved. Collected via a direct stars.list call using the connector's existing session credential (slackdump's archive mode does not call this method, but the credential it already captures does).

display.label: Your starred and saved items
display.detail: Messages, files, and channels you starred or saved in Slack.

streams.name: user_groups
streams.description: Workspace user groups (@engineering, @design, etc.): handle, description, members. Collected via a direct usergroups.list call using the connector's existing session credential (slackdump's archive mode does not call this method, but the credential it already captures does).

display.label: Slack user groups
display.detail: Group handles like @engineering, their names, descriptions, and membership.

streams.name: reminders
streams.description: User-owned Slack reminders. Collected via a direct reminders.list call using the connector's existing session credential (slackdump's archive mode does not call this method, but the credential it already captures does).

display.label: Your Slack reminders
display.detail: Reminders you set in Slack: text, trigger time, target user or channel, completion state.

streams.name: dm_read_states
streams.description: Per-DM last_read timestamps (conversations.info last_read / unread_count_display). Collected via direct conversations.info calls scoped to DM/MPIM channels, using the connector's existing session credential (slackdump's archive mode strips these fields, but the credential it already captures can call the method directly).

display.label: DM read states
display.detail: For each direct message or multi-party DM, the last message timestamp the user has read and the unread count.
